front: According to B V Ramana, what are the four axioms that define a group?
back: Closure, Associativity, Identity Element, Inverse Element.
A group (G, *) is called an Abelian (or commutative) group if it satisfies the commutative law: a * b = b * a for all a, b in G.

front: What is the definition of the 'order of a group'?
back: The order of a group (G, *) is the number of elements in G, denoted by |G|. If |G| is finite, it is a finite group; otherwise, it is an infinite group.

front: State Lagrange's Theorem for finite groups.
back: The order (number of elements) of every subgroup H of a finite group G divides the order of G.
A group G is called cyclic if there exists an element 'a' in G such that every element of G is a power of 'a' (i.e., G = {a^n | n is an integer}). The element 'a' is called a generator of G.
